feature.name: Web Speech API: Unspoken Punctuation
feature.summary: Adds a boolean unspokenPunctuation attribute to SpeechRecognition; when true the recognition engine auto-infers punctuation from pauses/grammar/prosody. Default false for backward compatibility.
feature.specMaturity: Draft Community Group Report (live spec) vs Working draft (ChromeStatus)

intake.finding: Well-formed intake. The ChromeStatus entry (created 2026-05-19 by evliu@google.com) names a concrete developer problem (raw unpunctuated transcripts force downstream NLP), a user benefit (natural continuous speech without dictating punctuation), target segments (dictation/transcription/conversational assistants), a single owner (Evan Liu, also spec editor), Blink component Blink>Speech, and a stated partner driver (Google Meet). Explainer, spec, intent-to-ship thread, tracking bug and sample all linked. Enterprise impact is negligible (1).
intake.limitations: Adoption expectation is scoped to a single internal partner (Google Meet within 12 months); no representative external developer-demand survey. Owner is concentrated in one engineer who is also the spec editor.

incubation.finding: The developer problem is real and recurring with independent corroboration: a 2018 StackOverflow question 'How to add punctuation to Web Speech API?' (4 upvotes, ~3k views) whose 2024 answer notes 'Microsoft Edge adds punctuations, Chrome doesn't'; a react-speech-recognition discussion that punctuation is 'hit-or-miss across the different speech APIs'. The explainer documents use cases, rejects the 'enable by default' alternative on backward-compatibility grounds, and cites industry precedent (Azure Speech, Google Cloud STT) that auto-punctuation is a configurable option rather than a forced default. This is a defensible, narrowly-scoped browser intervention.
incubation.limitations: Demand evidence is anecdotal/competitive-gap, not a representative survey; the only named adopter is an internal Google product. No evidence of seeking counterevidence (e.g., developers who prefer raw output only).

prototype.finding: A working prototype and demo exist (speech.evanliu.com 'Web Speech API Playground' exposes an Unspoken Punctuation toggle alongside on-device, quality and contextual-biasing options). The spec PR (#188) was merged: the unspokenPunctuation attribute is now normatively defined in the Web Speech API. The API surface is minimal (one boolean on an existing interface, default false), so framework/server integration friction is structurally low. The feature reached a flag-based developer trial.
prototype.limitations: Demo is owner-authored on a personal domain; no evidence of independent/third-party friction testing or compatibility spikes before the developer trial. Behavior is implementation-dependent (platform/OS speech engine).

developerTrials.finding: The captured launch event is a developer trial behind a flag in M150, and the feature has reached MDN/press visibility. However, there is no public evidence of a structured trial cohort: devtrial_instructions is null, there is no origin trial and no feedback-submission URL, and no attributable methodology, recruitment, representativeness, task-completion, or friction findings are published. The only observable external feedback channels are the public GitHub spec issues and the standards-position issues.
developerTrials.limitations: Cannot confirm who tried the feature, on which platforms/languages, or what they found; no published trial readout. Benefit/accuracy claims are therefore unvalidated by representative developer evidence.

wideReview.finding: Significant readiness/accuracy problems versus the ChromeStatus record. Mozilla is recorded as 'Positive' (ff_views=3) sourced from an informal comment by padenot on web-speech-api#187, but the FORMAL mozilla/standards-positions#1416 is Open with status 'Needs proposed position' (i.e., pending, not positive), and MDN BCD lists Firefox=No support. WebKit/standards-positions#678 is Open with no assignee/position (ChromeStatus 'No signal' is accurate). No TAG design review was requested (both vendor templates show 'TAG Design Review URL: No response'). The spec is a 'Draft Community Group Report' (21 May 2026) edited by the implementer, lower maturity than ChromeStatus's 'Working draft / Working Group' label implies. 'Web dev: Positive' is attributable to an internal partner (Google Meet). At this point the capability is single-engine.
wideReview.limitations: Could not retrieve the literal text of padenot's comment (#187) or the full standards-position deliberation; conclusion rests on issue state, labels and MDN BCD. Vendor positions may still move.

experiment.finding: No Origin Trial was conducted or planned (origintrial=false; ot fields null). The launch path is flag-based developer trial (M150) -> beta (M151) -> intended stable, with no field experiment. This is a defensible structural choice for a minimal additive opt-in boolean attribute, but the phase is genuinely not part of this feature's path.
experiment.limitations: Because behavior is ML/implementation-dependent and language/accent sensitive, field evidence on real-world punctuation accuracy would have had genuine value; its absence is an untested risk rather than confirmation of safety.

prepareToShip.finding: An intent-to-ship thread exists (stage 160, desktop target 151). The API is minimal, MDN compatibility data is already published and accurate, a sample exists, and enterprise impact is negligible. However, as of the cached record (accurate_as_of 2026-06-25) security_review_status and privacy_review_status were both still 'Pending', the formal Mozilla position is unresolved, there is no TAG review, and doc_links is empty (MDN/MDN-BCD stand in for reference docs).
prepareToShip.limitations: Full intent-thread content could not be retrieved (groups.google msgid URL was rejected by the reader); relied on ChromeStatus stage metadata plus the activity-page summary ('It simply adds a boolean attribute... It shifts the burden of adding punctuation'). Whether security/privacy reviews cleared before branch is unconfirmed from public evidence.

release.finding: Release communication has begun for the pre-stable milestones: the M150 developer trial is the captured event, and Chrome 151 Beta includes the feature with accurate, positive press coverage (Digital Trends, 2026-07-04) and accurate MDN compat data. But the feature had NOT reached stable/general availability as of the record (is_released=false; browsers.chrome.status='Proposed' as of 2026-06-25). Coordinated stable launch, release notes, and post-launch monitoring are not yet observable.
release.limitations: Stable M151 timing and any GA comms/incidents are outside the available evidence window; is_releasenotes_publish_ready=false in the record.

adoption.finding: A WebDX web-feature-family counter exists for the SpeechRecognition property (bucket_id=63; mappingStatus web-feature-family-counter; confidence declared-family), but there is NO exact counter for the unspokenPunctuation attribute (webdx_usecounter_enum=null), so the family percentage is NOT evidence of this attribute's adoption. The family counter has fired since 2024-06-10 — predating this feature — and is at a low, roughly flat baseline: 0.00029795% of measured Chrome HTTP/HTTPS page loads (2026-07-20), having peaked at 0.00139551% (2024-12-10) and first appeared at 0.000285% (2024-06-10); at the M150 dev-trial stable reference date (2026-06-30) it read 0.00033809%. day30/day90/day180 launch checkpoints are not yet available, and M151 stable is not captured as a checkpoint. This indicates the parent SpeechRecognition API itself is used on only a tiny share of page loads, so the addressable surface for the new attribute is small; it does not show whether any of those uses set unspokenPunctuation=true. No attributable third-party production deployment is evidenced (only the stated Google Meet intent within 12 months); no framework/library/tool integration evidence found.
adoption.limitations: Family counter is not an exact adoption measure for the attribute; the same low page-load share could include zero uses of unspokenPunctuation. No unique-site, production, retention, task-success, user-benefit, or numeric-target evidence. day30/90/180 not yet available; M151 stable not captured as a launch checkpoint.

support.finding: No public support issues, troubleshooting material, known regressions, or recurrence data exist yet (feature pre-stable). debuggability is recorded as 'None required'; the tracking bug 514764702 is the implementation bug (content not retrieved).
support.limitations: Pre-stable; support burden cannot be assessed. Revisit post-GA.

deprecation.finding: Phase 10 (deprecation/removal) does not apply: this is a brand-new, opt-in, additive boolean attribute (default false) being introduced, not a feature being restricted, replaced or removed. No removal, reverse trial, or migration is in scope.
deprecation.limitations: Structurally inapplicable at this stage; not used to hide an untested dimension.

developerValue.finding: Addresses a real, multi-year, independently documented developer pain (raw unpunctuated Web Speech transcripts forcing client/server NLP) with a minimal, backward-compatible, opt-in API that follows established industry precedent (Azure, Google Cloud STT treat auto-punctuation as configurable). Default false protects existing apps; the alternative of enabling by default was explicitly rejected on compatibility grounds.
developerValue.limitations: Demand evidence is anecdotal/competitive-gap rather than representative; sole named adopter is internal (Google Meet).

endUserImpact.finding: Plausible positive user impact (lower cognitive load, natural conversational dictation without saying 'comma'/'period'; accessibility/transcription benefit), but unverified by user research. Because punctuation/capitalization is implementation-dependent (platform speech engine) and the attribute defaults to false, end users only benefit where developers opt in, and accuracy/equity across accents, dialects and languages is not evidenced. Default-false also limits immediate user reach.
endUserImpact.limitations: No user-study, accuracy, or cross-language/accent evaluation in public evidence; benefit is hypothetical pending real use.

adoption.finding: Remains unscored. No exact counter exists for the unspokenPunctuation attribute — only the broader SpeechRecognition family counter (bucket_id=63), which is not attributable to this attribute and predates it — so the attribute-specific level/trend is unmeasured. There is no attributable third-party production deployment and no retention evidence. The only stated expectation is the Google Meet intent within 12 months (partner interest, not a numeric usage target and not a verified deployment), so there is no threshold against which to score. For context, the SpeechRecognition family counter sits at ~0.0003% of measured Chrome page loads (0.00029795% on 2026-07-20; 0.00033809% at the M150 dev-trial stable reference date 2026-06-30; day30/90/180 null), indicating a very small addressable surface; that figure does not measure this attribute.
adoption.limitations: No exact feature-level counter; the family counter is not attributable to the attribute. No unique-site, production, retention, task-success, user-benefit, or numeric-target evidence. Unscored because there is no predeclared numeric target and no corroborating production/retention evidence — not because usage is presumed low or high.

interoperability.finding: Currently a single-engine capability: Chrome 151 and Edge 151 (both Chromium) only per MDN BCD; Firefox=No, Safari=No, Chrome Android=No. Not Baseline-eligible; spec is a CG draft edited by the implementer; no TAG review; formal Mozilla position pending (not the 'Positive' recorded in ChromeStatus) and WebKit has no signal. Mitigating signal: Mozilla informal comment is non-negative, suggesting a possible future path.
interoperability.limitations: Very early; vendor positions and implementations may change, but at the evidence cutoff there is no second-engine implementation and no resolved positive standards position.

implementationQuality.finding: The API is minimal, safe and backward-compatible and the spec was merged with normative text; WPT is flagged as in-scope. Counterpoints: the core behavior (probabilistic punctuation/capitalization) is implementation-dependent and not deterministically testable, so conformance coverage of the actual behavior is effectively unobservable; coverage is desktop-only (Mac/Win/Linux) with no Android/WebView/iOS; implementation requires embedder support; and ownership/spec-editing is concentrated in a single engineer.
implementationQuality.limitations: No access to WPT specifics for this attribute; platform/quality behavior across OS speech engines unverified.

evidenceAndCommunication.finding: Explainer is clear and balanced (goals, non-goals, alternatives, backward-compat, i18n and capitalization caveats); MDN compat data and the Digital Trends article accurately reflect beta/experimental status; the adoption expectation is honestly scoped to one partner. Against this, the ChromeStatus record materially overstates Mozilla's position (recorded 'Positive' from an informal comment while the formal standards-position issue is still pending) and slightly overstates standards maturity ('Working draft / Working Group' vs the live 'Draft Community Group Report').
evidenceAndCommunication.limitations: Could not retrieve the literal Mozilla comment or full intent-thread text to fully reconcile the 'Positive' classification.

support.finding: No support burden evidence yet (pre-stable); debuggability recorded as 'None required'.

overall.finding: An early-stage, well-motivated, minimal additive capability with a genuinely validated developer problem and a clean opt-in API, shipping Chrome-first (M150 dev trial; M151 beta; stable target M151). It is too early to verdict adoption: there is no exact counter for the attribute, and the only available telemetry (the SpeechRecognition family counter, bucket_id=63) sits at ~0.0003% of measured Chrome page loads — a small addressable surface — but predates and is not attributable to unspokenPunctuation. End-user impact and support also remain unscored. Principal risks: Chrome-only interop with no resolved positive standards position and no TAG review; a ChromeStatus record that overstates Mozilla's position; implementation-dependent ML behavior with no field/accuracy evidence; no Origin Trial or structured cohort feedback; pending security/privacy reviews at the time of the record; and single-owner concentration (owner is also spec editor).
overall.limitations: Overall is provisional; a post-stable revision with an exact attribute counter, partner verification and resolved reviews is required before a firmer verdict.

supports: Feature metadata, owner, stages, intent-to-ship for M151, dev-trial M150, security/privacy review 'Pending', adoption expectation (Google Meet), finch name, no OT, no exact use counter for the attribute (webdx_usecounter_enum=null; web_feature speech-recognition), ff_views=3/safari_views=5/web_dev_views=2
sources.limitations: Record can lag live state; some fields (intent_thread content, review outcomes) not inline.

supports: Goals, use cases, alternatives (enable-by-default rejected for backward compat), industry precedent (Azure/Google Cloud), default false, capitalization/i18n caveats
sources.limitations: Author is also the feature owner/implementer.

sources.publisher: W3C / WebAudio Community Group spec (Editor: Evan Liu, Google)
supports: Normative unspokenPunctuation attribute text, default false; spec is 'Draft Community Group Report'; security/privacy section is generic; cluster of new on-device speech features (processLocally, phrases, quality enum)
sources.limitations: CG draft, low formal maturity; security/privacy text not specific to this attribute.

supports: Original proposal by evanbliu, closed and linked to spec PR #188; issue referenced by ChromeStatus as basis for Mozilla 'Positive' (padenot comment)
sources.limitations: Comment text (incl. padenot's) not rendered by reader; only issue body/state retrieved.

sources.limitations: Position may change; only issue state/labels retrieved, not deliberation.

supports: WebKit review Open with no assignee/labels/position; confirms 'No signal'; both vendor templates show no TAG review requested
unspokenPunctuation: Experimental; Chrome 151 + Edge 151 Preview support; Firefox No; Safari No; Chrome Android No
sources.limitations: BCD reflects current stable/beta state and updates over time.

supports: Long-standing developer pain with raw unpunctuated Web Speech output; 2024 answer notes 'Microsoft Edge adds punctuations, Chrome doesn't'; workarounds require server-side NLP
Independent confirmation that punctuation is 'hit-or-miss across the different speech APIs' and Azure polyfill compared
sources.limitations: Snippet from search result; full thread not retrieved.

supports: Working playground exposing Unspoken Punctuation toggle alongside on-device, quality and contextual-biasing options
sources.limitations: Owner-authored demo, not an independent case study.

supports: Chrome 151 Beta includes the feature; positive framing; notes experimental status and that developer adoption will determine reach
sources.limitations: Single consumer-tech press item; restates Google's framing.

supports: Intent-to-ship thread referenced by ChromeStatus stage 160; summary via activity page: 'It simply adds a boolean attribute... shifts the burden of adding punctuation'
sources.limitations: Full thread content not directly retrieved (msgid URL rejected by reader); summary taken from ChromeStatus activity/gate snippet.
Bug content not retrieved; referenced from ChromeStatus record.

supports: Intent-thread summary quote used to corroborate prepareToShip finding
api.SpeechRecognition.unspokenPunctuation added to browser-compat-data, tracking its introduction
sources.limitations: Changelog entry only; no behavioral detail.

sources.publisher: Chrome Platform Status (WebDX web-feature popularity timeline, bucket_id=63 / SpeechRecognition)
supports: WebDX web-feature-family counter for the SpeechRecognition property (bucket_id=63; mappingStatus web-feature-family-counter; confidence declared-family); latest snapshot 2026-07-20 day_percentage 0.00029795%; at the M150 dev-trial stable reference date 2026-06-30 = 0.00033809%; first positive 2024-06-10 = 0.000285%; peak 2024-12-10 = 0.00139551%; day30/day90/day180 = null
Counter measures the SpeechRecognition property family, NOT the unspokenPunctuation attribute; no exact feature-level counter exists (webdx_usecounter_enum=null)
Counter has fired since 2024-06-10, predating this feature's M150 dev trial, i.e., baseline parent-API usage independent of the attribute
sources.limitations: Family counter (SpeechRecognition) covers all SpeechRecognition usage; firing does not imply unspokenPunctuation was set true. Values are the share of measured Chrome HTTP/HTTPS page loads where the counter fired — not unique developers, sites, users, tasks, satisfaction, production deployment, or end-user benefit. day30/day90/day180 not yet available; M151 stable not captured as a launch checkpoint.

successes: Genuinely validated, multi-year developer problem with independent corroboration (StackOverflow 2018, react-speech-recognition) and a clear competitive gap vs Edge/Azure
Minimal, backward-compatible, opt-in API (default false) following industry precedent; explicit rejection of the breaking 'enable by default' alternative
Spec merged with normative attribute text; working owner-authored demo; accurate MDN compat data and accurate (positive) beta press coverage (Digital Trends)
Honest scoping of the adoption expectation to a single named partner rather than broad claims
failures: ChromeStatus materially overstates Mozilla's position as 'Positive' from an informal comment while the formal mozilla/standards-positions#1416 is still pending (and Firefox shows No support in MDN BCD)
No TAG design review requested; spec is a low-maturity CG draft edited by the implementer
No Origin Trial and no public developer-trial cohort methodology/feedback, so the core behavior is unvalidated by representative evidence

No EXACT use counter for the unspokenPunctuation attribute — only a broader SpeechRecognition family counter (bucket_id=63) exists, which is not attributable to this attribute and predates it, so attribute-level adoption cannot be measured
Security and privacy reviews still 'Pending' in the cached record at intent-to-ship time
Cross-engine interop absent at launch: Chrome/Edge only; Firefox No, Safari No, not Baseline-eligible
counterfactuals: If a TAG review had been requested before intent-to-ship, the implementation-dependent punctuation/capitalization behavior (and any i18n, fingerprinting, or accuracy-equity implications) would likely have received formal scrutiny before stable
If the formal Mozilla position had been resolved rather than treating an informal comment as 'Positive', the interoperability path (and the ChromeStatus record) would be more defensible
If an Origin Trial or a structured, attributable developer cohort with feedback had been run, real-world punctuation accuracy across accents, dialects and languages would have surfaced before stable

If an EXACT use counter for the unspokenPunctuation attribute (not merely the SpeechRecognition family counter, bucket_id=63) had been allocated at dev-trial time, attribute-level post-stable adoption would be measurable today; webdx_usecounter_enum is currently null
If the single owner were not also the sole spec editor, an independent spec reviewer might have challenged the implementation-dependent, untestable normative behavior
skillImprovements: Phase 04 (wide review) rule: an informal comment on a spec issue must never be recorded as a formal vendor 'Positive' position; require resolving the actual standards-position repo state (Open/needs-position vs labeled position) and reconcile with MDN BCD before setting ff/safari views
Transition-packet check: TAG review status must be explicitly stated (requested, waived with justification, or absent) — never silently absent — before prepare-to-ship

Rule: when a feature's behavior is probabilistic/implementation-dependent (ML punctuation), require a field-evidence or accuracy-evaluation plan even for a 'minimal additive boolean' API, and a not-relevant experiment phase must carry the residual risk
Pre-ship check: security_review_status and privacy_review_status should not remain 'Pending' when intent-to-ship is filed; flag as a launch-readiness gap

Practice: allocate an EXACT UseCounter/webdx bucket for each new attribute (here unspokenPunctuation) at developer-trial time — a family counter (e.g., SpeechRecognition, bucket_id=63) is insufficient because it predates and conflates the new attribute with existing usage
Checklist item: flag owner-concentration risk (single owner also editing the spec) for bus-factor and editorial-bias review, and recommend a second spec reviewer
Evidence rule: prefer chromestatuslite.com server-rendered route or the WPT dashboard over JS-rendered chromestatus.com/activity when automated readers are used, to avoid empty retrieval

missingEvidence: Exact feature-level adoption/usage for the unspokenPunctuation attribute — only the broader SpeechRecognition family counter (bucket_id=63) exists, which is not attributable to the attribute; plus no unique-site, production, retention, or numeric-target evidence
Attributable third-party production deployment (only internal Google Meet intent recorded)
Second-engine implementation or resolved positive standards position (Chrome/Edge only; Mozilla formal position pending; WebKit no signal; no TAG review)
Developer-trial cohort methodology, recruitment, representativeness, feedback themes and friction results (none public)
Real-world punctuation/capitalization accuracy and equity across accents, dialects and languages (no field evidence)
Confirmed security and privacy review outcomes (both 'Pending' in cached record)
Full intent-to-ship thread content and literal text of padenot's comment on web-speech-api#187 (URLs not retrievable via reader; relied on metadata and snippets)
